title: Implement structured logging with automatic trace context inclusion
purpose: Provide a structured logging facility that automatically includes the active trace context in every log entry. All log output is structured key-value pairs â€” never free-form strings. This enables operators to search logs by trace_id or span_id and correlate log entries with distributed traces. The logging layer reads the current TraceContext from OBS-006 and injects trace_id and span_id fields into every emitted record.
task: Create crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs containing the structured logging types and the LogEntry struct. Define LogLevel, LogEntry (with timestamp, level, message, trace context, and arbitrary key-value fields), and a LogEmitter trait for output sinks. Provide a global log function that captures the current trace context automatically. All entries are structured â€” no free-form string interpolation in the logging API.

requirements.title: Define LogLevel enum
requirements.spec: LogLevel SHALL have exactly five variants: Trace, Debug, Info, Warn, Error, ordered by increasing severity. LogLevel SHALL derive Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord. LogLevel SHALL NOT include Fatal or Off â€” those are not log levels.
acceptance: LogLevel has exactly five variants: Trace, Debug, Info, Warn, Error
LogLevel derives Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord
LogLevel::Trace < LogLevel::Debug < LogLevel::Info < LogLevel::Warn < LogLevel::Error

files: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:new â€” define the new logging module and keep it under the 500-line cap; tests can live at the bottom if total size remains safe
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-007.json:14-29 â€” exact LogLevel variants, derive list, and ordering acceptance
crates/liminal/src/tracing/mod.rs:1-9 â€” current tracing module style; only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports
Cargo.toml:18-27 â€” workspace lints deny clippy all/pedantic, unwrap_used, expect_used, and panic
context: No `logging.rs` exists yet under `crates/liminal/src/tracing`; this brief creates it rather than extending span/context files.

Enums in nearby tracing code derive only what they need: `TraceContext` derives `Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq`; `FinishedSpan` derives `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq`; follow explicit derives rather than adding extras.
Variant order alone can satisfy `Trace < Debug < Info < Warn < Error`; do not add discriminants unless needed, and do not add `Fatal` or `Off`.
Because public types must implement Debug and clippy pedantic is denied, add unit tests for ordering and avoid unused public helpers.
scout.approach: Create `pub enum LogLevel { Trace, Debug, Info, Warn, Error }` in the new logging module with exactly `#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord)]`. Add a small test asserting the ordering chain. Keep this definition near the top of `logging.rs` so later builder/emitter code can share it.
scout.notes: The crate currently depends on the external `tracing` crate in `crates/liminal/Cargo.toml`, but the brief boundary says not to use it for this API; implement `LogLevel` in `crate::tracing::logging`, not via the dependency.

requirements.title: Define LogEntry as a structured key-value record
requirements.spec: LogEntry SHALL contain: timestamp (Instant), level (LogLevel), target (module path as &'static str), message (String), trace_context (Option<TraceContext>), and fields (Vec<(String, LogValue)>). LogEntry SHALL implement Debug. LogValue SHALL be an enum supporting String, i64, u64, f64, and bool variants. LogEntry SHALL NOT contain any unstructured text field beyond message â€” all additional data goes in fields.
acceptance: LogEntry has fields: timestamp, level, target, message, trace_context, fields
LogEntry implements Debug
LogValue has variants: String, I64, U64, F64, Bool
No free-form text field exists beyond the message field

files: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:new â€” add `LogValue` and `LogEntry` here alongside `LogLevel`
crates/liminal/src/tracing/context.rs:3-39 â€” `TraceContext` is Copy/Debug and exposes `trace_id()`/`span_id()` accessors for log searchability
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-007.json:30-47 â€” exact required fields and `LogValue` variants
Cargo.toml:18-27 â€” no panics/unwraps/expects in production; tests are allowed but still run under denied lints unless locally justified
context: `LogEntry` should contain exactly the required structural fields: `timestamp: Instant`, `level: LogLevel`, `target: &'static str`, `message: String`, `trace_context: Option<TraceContext>`, and `fields: Vec<(String, LogValue)>`.

`LogValue` variants should be exactly `String(String)`, `I64(i64)`, `U64(u64)`, `F64(f64)`, and `Bool(bool)`; deriving `Clone`, `Debug`, and `PartialEq` will make tests and emitters easier while satisfying Debug.
The design says no extra free-form text beyond `message`; avoid fields such as `text`, `body`, `details`, or `args`.
Use `std::time::Instant`, matching span timing code in `tracing/span.rs:9` and tests around `span.start()`.
scout.approach: Define `LogValue` and `LogEntry` in `logging.rs`. Prefer public fields if acceptance checks are likely to construct/read entries directly, or provide accessors in addition if encapsulation is desired; either way, preserve the exact field names. `LogEntry` can derive `Debug` and likely `Clone`/`PartialEq` if tests need comparisons, though `Instant` supports these. Add tests that construct a `LogEntry` and pattern-match each `LogValue` variant.
scout.notes: `f64` prevents deriving `Eq` for `LogValue`; do not try to satisfy the global public-Debug convention by over-deriving traits that `f64` cannot support.

requirements.title: Automatic trace context inclusion in log entries
requirements.spec: When a log entry is created within a conversation context, the active TraceContext SHALL be automatically captured and included in the LogEntry's trace_context field. The trace_id and span_id SHALL be available as searchable fields. When no trace context is active, trace_context SHALL be None. Trace context capture SHALL NOT require the caller to pass the context explicitly.
acceptance: A log entry created within a conversation has trace_context == Some(ctx)
A log entry created outside a conversation has trace_context == None
trace_id and span_id from the trace context are accessible on the LogEntry
No explicit TraceContext parameter is required at the log call site

files: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:new â€” `LogEntryBuilder::build()` and any log emission function should call `current_trace_context()` automatically
crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:344-351 â€” public `current_trace_context()` reads the existing thread-local active trace context
crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:187-267 â€” `SpanGuard` sets/restores active context when conversation spans are entered/dropped
crates/liminal/src/tracing/context.rs:31-39 â€” accessors for trace_id/span_id that can be exposed/searchable without duplicating context state
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-007.json:49-65 â€” acceptance criteria for Some(ctx), None, searchable IDs, and no explicit parameter
context: The thread-local active context is already implemented by OBS-006 in `span.rs`; logging should read it rather than define a second TLS store.
`current_trace_context()` returns `Option<TraceContext>` by value because `TraceContext` is Copy, so capture is cheap and lock-free.
`SpanGuard::start_conversation` is the test harness to create an active conversation context; dropping the guard restores `None`.

Searchable IDs can be satisfied either through `entry.trace_context.map(|ctx| ctx.trace_id())`/`span_id()` or by adding convenience accessors on `LogEntry`; avoid duplicating `trace_id`/`span_id` into the `fields` vector unless tests/design explicitly require it.
scout.approach: Make `LogEntryBuilder::build()` populate `trace_context` from `crate::tracing::current_trace_context()` with no context parameter in `new()` or `field()`. Add tests that build outside a span and assert `None`, then build inside `let guard = SpanGuard::start_conversation("...")` and assert `Some(guard.context())`. Add `LogEntry::trace_id()` and `LogEntry::span_id()` convenience methods returning `Option<u128>`/`Option<u64>` if you want the â€śsearchable fieldsâ€ť acceptance to be direct without changing the required struct shape.
scout.notes: Do not import or use the external `tracing` crate despite the crate dependency; the module path name overlaps and can confuse imports. Use `crate::tracing::{current_trace_context, TraceContext}`.

requirements.title: Thread-local trace context storage for automatic capture
requirements.spec: The current TraceContext SHALL be stored in a thread-local variable that is set when a conversation span begins and cleared when it ends. The log entry constructor SHALL read this thread-local to populate trace_context. This mechanism SHALL be transparent to application code. SHALL NOT use a global Mutex for context storage â€” thread-local is lock-free.
acceptance: A thread-local TraceContext is set when entering a conversation span
The thread-local is cleared when leaving the conversation span
Log entries automatically read the thread-local for trace context
No Mutex or RwLock is used for context storage

files: crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:17-19 â€” existing `thread_local!` `ACTIVE_TRACE_CONTEXT: Cell<Option<TraceContext>>` storage
crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:230-257 â€” entering a `SpanGuard` replaces TLS with `Some(context)` and records previous context
crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:261-267 â€” `Drop` restores previous context when leaving a span
crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:344-351 â€” existing public read accessor plus private replace helper
crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:new â€” read the existing accessor only; do not create Mutex/RwLock context storage here
context: R4â€™s storage requirement is already met by OBS-006: a thread-local `Cell<Option<TraceContext>>` is set/cleared by `SpanGuard`.
The current implementation supports nesting by storing `previous_context` and restoring it, so logging tests should include nested spans only if needed, not reimplement nesting.
The acceptance says no Mutex/RwLock for context storage; `logging.rs` should not add any context-specific synchronization at all.
`metrics/registry.rs` uses RwLock for metrics state, but that pattern is not appropriate for trace context; avoid copying it for R4.
scout.approach: Treat `span.rs` as the owner of context storage. In `logging.rs`, add a small private helper such as `fn capture_trace_context() -> Option<TraceContext> { current_trace_context() }` if useful, and have builders call it during `build()`. Tests for R4 should exercise `SpanGuard` rather than exposing new setters: start a guard, build an entry, assert context; drop guard, build another entry, assert None; optionally assert source contains no context Mutex/RwLock through code review rather than runtime behavior.
scout.notes: The brief says modify `logging.rs` for R4, but the actual set/clear implementation is already in `span.rs`. Avoid moving TLS from `span.rs`; moving it would create churn and risk breaking OBS-006 behavior.

requirements.title: Define LogEmitter trait for output sinks
requirements.spec: LogEmitter SHALL be a trait with a single method: emit(entry: &LogEntry). This is the extension point for log output destinations (stderr, file, network). A NoopEmitter SHALL be provided that discards all entries. The active emitter SHALL be set globally via an atomic pointer. When no emitter is installed, logging SHALL be a no-op with zero overhead. LogEmitter SHALL require Send + Sync.
acceptance: LogEmitter trait has fn emit(&self, entry: &LogEntry)
NoopEmitter implements LogEmitter and discards entries
A global optional LogEmitter is set via atomic pointer
When no emitter is installed, log calls perform no work
LogEmitter: Send + Sync is required

files: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:new â€” define `LogEmitter`, `NoopEmitter`, global emitter storage, install/global accessors, and emission function
crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:13-15 â€” existing atomic marker + `OnceLock<Box<dyn SpanCollector>>` pattern for global sink install
crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:270-342 â€” `SpanCollector`, `NoopCollector`, install error, enabled fast path, and global accessor pattern to mirror
crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:184-209 â€” alternative global registry pattern using an atomic enabled flag before accessing `OnceLock`
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-007.json:85-102 â€” exact emitter trait and no-op requirements
context: The span collector already uses `AtomicPtr<()>` as a cheap installed marker plus `OnceLock<Box<dyn SpanCollector>>`; this is the closest precedent for the required global optional `LogEmitter` atomic pointer behavior.

`SpanCollector` is `std::fmt::Debug + Send + Sync + 'static`; `LogEmitter` must at least be `Debug + Send + Sync + 'static` if you want all public types and trait objects to satisfy Debug conventions.
When no emitter is installed, the hot log call should return before constructing `LogEntry` or allocating fields; the fast path should be a single atomic load/null check.
OnceLock means installation can be one-shot; if you expose install functions, define a Debug error type implementing `Display` and `Error`, mirroring `SpanCollectorInstallError`.
scout.approach: Define `pub trait LogEmitter: std::fmt::Debug + Send + Sync + 'static { fn emit(&self, entry: &LogEntry); }` and `#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Default)] pub struct NoopEmitter;`. For global storage, mirror `SPAN_COLLECTOR_MARKER`/`GLOBAL_SPAN_COLLECTOR`: an atomic marker/pointer for enabled check and a `OnceLock<Box<dyn LogEmitter>>` for ownership. Provide `install_log_emitter`/`install_boxed_log_emitter`, `log_emitter_enabled()`, `global_log_emitter()`, and an emission API that checks enabled before building/emitting.
scout.notes: A literal atomic pointer to the trait object is hard because fat pointers are not directly atomic. The existing code solves this with an atomic thin marker plus `OnceLock` for the actual trait object; use that convention unless acceptance tests demand a differently named atomic. Do not make `NoopEmitter` the default installed emitter, because â€śno emitter installedâ€ť must be a true no-op fast path.

requirements.title: Provide structured log construction API
requirements.spec: A LogEntryBuilder SHALL provide a fluent API for constructing log entries with typed key-value fields. The builder SHALL enforce that all entries are structured: field(key, value) adds a typed field. The builder SHALL automatically capture the current trace context. The builder SHALL NOT accept format strings or free-form interpolation â€” message is a static or pre-formatted string, additional data goes in fields.
acceptance: LogEntryBuilder::new(level, target, message) creates a builder
builder.field("user_id", LogValue::String(...)) adds a structured field
builder.build() returns a LogEntry with the current trace context
No format! or write! macro usage in the builder API

files: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:new â€” implement `LogEntryBuilder` and its tests here
crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:344-351 â€” builder should call `current_trace_context()` during `build()`
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-007.json:104-120 â€” fluent API and no format/write macro acceptance
crates/liminal/src/tracing/context.rs:31-39 â€” optional convenience methods can expose trace/span IDs on resulting entries
context: There are no existing builder types in `crates/liminal/src`, so choose a simple owned builder style consistent with Rust conventions.
`field("user_id", LogValue::String(...))` should add to `Vec<(String, LogValue)>`; use `key.into()` to support `&str` without requiring callers to allocate manually.
The API can accept `message: impl Into<String>` for the required `LogEntryBuilder::new(level, target, message)` without accepting format strings itself.
Avoid `format!` or `write!` inside the builder implementation; caller-supplied pre-formatted strings are allowed by the spec, but builder should not expose interpolation helpers.
scout.approach: Implement `pub struct LogEntryBuilder { level, target, message, fields }` with `pub fn new(level: LogLevel, target: &'static str, message: impl Into<String>) -> Self`, `pub fn field(mut self, key: impl Into<String>, value: LogValue) -> Self`, and `pub fn build(self) -> LogEntry`. `build()` should set `timestamp: Instant::now()` and `trace_context: current_trace_context()`. Add tests for fluent chaining, typed fields, automatic context capture, and absence of explicit context parameters at call sites.
scout.notes: If you later add a convenience `log(level, target, message)` function for emission, keep allocation avoidance in R7 separate: the builder itself necessarily owns `String` and `Vec`, so the filtered emission path must check level/emitter before creating a builder/entry.

requirements.title: Level-filtered emission
requirements.spec: The global log configuration SHALL support a minimum LogLevel filter. Log entries below the minimum level SHALL be discarded before constructing the LogEntry, avoiding allocation. The filter check SHALL be a single atomic load. SHALL NOT impose a default minimum level â€” the filter is set explicitly by the caller.
acceptance: Setting minimum level to Info causes Trace and Debug entries to be discarded
Discarded entries do not allocate a LogEntry or Vec<(String, LogValue)>
The level check is a single atomic load
No default minimum level is set â€” all levels are emitted until a filter is installed

files: crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:new â€” implement atomic min-level filter and filtered emission helper
crates/liminal/src/tracing/span.rs:330-342 â€” existing enabled check performs an atomic load before accessing global collector
crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:197-209 â€” existing `metrics_enabled()`/`global_registry()` fast-path shape
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-007.json:122-138 â€” no default minimum level and discard-before-allocation acceptance
Cargo.toml:18-27 â€” strict clippy may flag atomic ordering or unnecessary casts; keep implementation minimal
context: No default minimum filter means all levels should be eligible until the caller explicitly installs a minimum. Represent unset separately from `Trace`; otherwise a default `Trace` filter may technically work behaviorally but violates the explicit unset requirement.
A single atomic load can encode unset plus five levels in an `AtomicU8`, e.g. `0 = unset`, `1..=5 = Trace..Error`.

Filtering below min must happen before constructing `LogEntry`, `String`, or `Vec`; this implies a function like `log_enabled(level)` and an emission helper that takes a closure/builder only after passing checks.
The emitter-installed check and min-level filter are separate. For true no-op with no emitter, check emitter first, then min-level, then build.
scout.approach: Add `static MIN_LOG_LEVEL: AtomicU8 = AtomicU8::new(0)` and methods/functions such as `set_min_log_level(level: LogLevel)` and `min_log_level() -> Option<LogLevel>`. Implement `level_enabled(level)` with exactly one `load(Ordering::Acquire)` from the min-level atomic and compare only if nonzero. For allocation-free discard, provide an emission API like `emit_log(level, target, message, fields_fn)` or a lower-level `try_emit(builder_factory)` that checks `global_log_emitter().is_none()` and `level_enabled(level)` before constructing the builder/entry; tests can use an allocation-counting pattern if needed, but code review should ensure construction happens after checks.
scout.notes: Do not set a default minimum level during emitter installation. Also be careful not to call `message.into()` before the level/emitter checks in any public convenience logging function, because that would allocate for discarded entries.

requirements.title: Add logging module to tracing mod.rs
requirements.spec: crates/liminal/src/tracing/mod.rs SHALL declare pub mod logging and re-export LogLevel, LogEntry, LogValue, LogEmitter, and LogEntryBuilder.
acceptance: tracing/mod.rs contains pub mod logging
LogLevel, LogEntry, LogValue, LogEmitter, LogEntryBuilder are re-exported
cargo check -p liminal passes

files: crates/liminal/src/tracing/mod.rs:1-9 â€” add `pub mod logging;` and re-export requested logging types while preserving mod.rs-only-declarations/re-exports rule
crates/liminal/src/tracing/logging.rs:new â€” source of `LogLevel`, `LogEntry`, `LogValue`, `LogEmitter`, `LogEntryBuilder` for re-export
crates/liminal/src/lib.rs:12-19 â€” crate root currently re-exports only `TraceContext`; no requirement to re-export logging at crate root
crates/liminal/src/metrics/mod.rs:1-14 â€” module re-export style to mirror for multi-item grouped public exports
docs/design/observability/briefs/OBS-007.json:140-147 â€” exact R8 acceptance and cargo check requirement
context: `tracing/mod.rs` currently contains only `pub mod` declarations and `pub use` re-exports, which matches CN3; keep it that way.
Add logging to the existing tracing namespace; do not create a top-level `logging` module in `lib.rs` unless a later brief asks for it.
The existing `pub use span::{...}` group includes functions as well as types; add a separate `pub use logging::{LogEmitter, LogEntry, LogEntryBuilder, LogLevel, LogValue};` group for clarity.
`cargo check -p liminal` must pass with Rust 2024 and all workspace lints inherited by the crate.
scout.approach: After creating `logging.rs`, edit `tracing/mod.rs` to include `pub mod logging;` with the other module declarations and add a grouped re-export for the five required public items. Leave `lib.rs` unchanged unless tests reveal consumers need crate-root exports. Run formatting and `cargo check -p liminal`; for full confidence run clippy too because the workspace denies many pedantic lints.
scout.notes: If implementation adds install/config helpers or `NoopEmitter`, consider re-exporting them too for usability, but R8 only requires the five named items; avoid over-changing public surface if acceptance tests are narrow.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement any metrics collection â€” that is OBS-001 through OBS-005.
SHALL NOT implement trace context or span types â€” that is OBS-006. This brief only reads the trace context.
SHALL NOT implement log output formatting (JSON, logfmt) â€” those are LogEmitter implementations, not the logging core.
SHALL NOT use the log crate or tracing crate â€” the structured logging API is self-contained.
SHALL NOT allow free-form string interpolation in the logging API â€” all data must be structured fields.
SHALL NOT impose a default log level filter.
verification: cargo clippy -p liminal -- -D warnings passes clean.
LogLevel ordering is Trace < Debug < Info < Warn < Error.
Log entries within a conversation automatically include trace_id and span_id.
Log entries outside a conversation have trace_context == None.
No Mutex or RwLock is used for trace context storage.
Logging is a no-op when no LogEmitter is installed.
